business_type: Shopify skincare brand, ~40 orders/day, ships direct-to-consumer across US and Canada
current_role: Solo founder — owns product, marketing, and all customer operations
weekly_tasks_text: Answer customer support emails about order status, shipping delays, wrong items, and return requests; process refunds and exchanges in Shopify admin; chase the fulfillment partner when orders are delayed or missing; write and schedule 3-4 Instagram posts and Stories per week; send one promotional email per week to a list of ~4,000; review ad spend and ROAS on Meta daily; restock inventory when a SKU drops below threshold; reply to Instagram DMs and comments; update product pages when descriptions or ingredients change.
current_tools_text: Shopify for storefront and order management, Gmail for support (no helpdesk), Meta Ads Manager, Klaviyo for email, Later for social scheduling, Canva for creative, Notion for notes, a 3PL for fulfillment.
top_time_sinks_text: Customer support inbox is the biggest drain — at 40 orders/day I get 80-120 support emails per week: mostly 'where is my order', 'I want to return this', 'I got the wrong item'. Each one requires opening the order in Shopify, checking the 3PL tracking, and writing a reply. I spend 4-6 hours a week just in Gmail. Social content is next — filming, editing, writing captions, and scheduling each post takes 2-3 hours. Weekly email takes another 2 hours. Meta ad review is 30-40 min/day but I could batch it.
error_sensitive_areas_text: Issuing refunds and replacements — getting the order or amount wrong costs money directly. Shipping address corrections — if I catch an address error after the 3PL picks up the package there is nothing I can do. Return eligibility — our policy has exceptions for opened skincare and I can't automate that judgment. Ingredient claims on product pages — regulatory risk if copy is wrong.
customer_facing_areas_text: Support email replies; Shopify order confirmation and shipping notification copy; Instagram posts and DMs; weekly promotional email; product page descriptions.
primary_goal_text: Cut the time I spend in the support inbox from 4-6 hours a week to under 1 hour, so I can put that time into ad creative and product development.
things_owner_refuses_to_automate_text: Final decision on return eligibility for edge cases. Any reply to an angry or distressed customer — I want those to come from me. Ingredient or safety claims on product pages. Decisions about ad budget changes over $50.
measurement_context_text: Task: customer support inbox. I receive roughly 80-120 support emails per week right now. I'd estimate 70% are one of three types: order status ('where is my package'), return requests ('I want to return this, what do I do'), and wrong item reports ('I got X but ordered Y'). Each of these takes me 3-5 minutes: find the order in Shopify, check 3PL tracking or lodge a ticket, write a reply. The other 30% need judgment — damage claims, refund disputes, unusual situations — and I keep those for myself. If I only had to handle the 30% judgment cases and AI drafted the other 70%, I'd go from ~5 hours/week to roughly 1.5 hours. 'This worked' for me means: I open a draft, it has the right order number, correct tracking link, and a reply in my brand tone, and I hit send in under 60 seconds. I'd stop using it if more than 1 in 10 drafts pulls the wrong order or has the tracking link wrong — at that point I'm spending more time verifying than I saved.
